title: ग्रह शान्ति मन्त्र
devanagariText:
॥ श्री ग्रह शान्ति मन्त्र ॥

ॐ नमो भगवते वासुदेवाय।
सर्वग्रहाणां शान्तये ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥

॥ सूर्य शान्ति मन्त्र ॥
ॐ ह्रां ह्रीं ह्रौं सः सूर्याय नमः।
जपाकुसुमसङ्काशं काश्यपेयं महद्द्युतिम्।
तमोऽरिं सर्वपापघ्नं प्रणतोऽस्मि दिवाकरम्॥१॥

॥ चन्द्र शान्ति मन्त्र ॥
ॐ श्रां श्रीं श्रौं सः चन्द्राय नमः।
दधिशङ्खतुषाराभं क्षीरोदार्णवसम्भवम्।
नमामि शशिनं सोमं शम्भोर्मुकुटभूषणम्॥२॥

॥ मङ्गल शान्ति मन्त्र ॥
ॐ क्रां क्रीं क्रौं सः भौमाय नमः।
धरणीगर्भसम्भूतं विद्युत्कान्तिसमप्रभम्।
कुमारं शक्तिहस्तं तं मङ्गलं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥३॥

॥ बुध शान्ति मन्त्र ॥
ॐ ब्रां ब्रीं ब्रौं सः बुधाय नमः।
प्रियङ्गुकलिकाश्यामं रूपेणाप्रतिमं बुधम्।
सौम्यं सौम्यगुणोपेतं तं बुधं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥४॥

॥ गुरु शान्ति मन्त्र ॥
ॐ ग्रां ग्रीं ग्रौं सः गुरवे नमः।
देवानां च ऋषीणां च गुरुं काञ्चनसन्निभम्।
बुद्धिभूतं त्रिलोकेशं तं नमामि बृहस्पतिम्॥५॥

॥ शुक्र शान्ति मन्त्र ॥
ॐ द्रां द्रीं द्रौं सः शुक्राय नमः।
हिमकुन्दमृणालाभं दैत्यानां परमं गुरुम्।
सर्वशास्त्रप्रवक्तारं भार्गवं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥६॥

॥ शनि शान्ति मन्त्र ॥
ॐ प्रां प्रीं प्रौं सः शनैश्चराय नमः।
नीलाञ्जनसमाभासं रविपुत्रं यमाग्रजम्।
छायामार्तण्डसम्भूतं तं नमामि शनैश्चरम्॥७॥

॥ राहु शान्ति मन्त्र ॥
ॐ भ्रां भ्रीं भ्रौं सः राहवे नमः।
अर्धकायं महावीर्यं चन्द्रादित्यविमर्दनम्।
सिंहिकागर्भसम्भूतं तं राहुं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥८॥

॥ केतु शान्ति मन्त्र ॥
ॐ स्रां स्रीं स्रौं सः केतवे नमः।
पलाशपुष्पसङ्काशं तारकाग्रहमस्तकम्।
रौद्रं रौद्रात्मकं घोरं तं केतुं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥९॥

॥ सामूहिक शान्ति मन्त्र ॥
ब्रह्मा मुरारिस्त्रिपुरान्तकारी
भानुः शशी भूमिसुतो बुधश्च।
गुरुश्च शुक्रः शनिराहुकेतवः
सर्वे ग्रहाः शान्तिकराः भवन्तु॥१०॥

ॐ सर्वग्रहेभ्यो नमः। ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥

॥ इति श्री ग्रह शान्ति मन्त्र सम्पूर्णः ॥
transliteration:
|| Shrī Graha Śānti Mantra ||

Om Namo Bhagavate Vāsudevāya.
Sarvagrāhāṇāṁ śāntaye Om Śāntiḥ Śāntiḥ Śāntiḥ.

|| Sūrya Śānti Mantra ||
Om Hrāṁ Hrīṁ Hrauṁ Saḥ Sūryāya Namaḥ.
Japākusumasaṅkāśaṁ Kāśyapeyaṁ mahaddyutim.
Tamo'riṁ sarvapāpaghnaṁ praṇato'smi Divākaram.||1||

|| Chandra Śānti Mantra ||
Om Śrāṁ Śrīṁ Śrauṁ Saḥ Chandrāya Namaḥ.
Dadhiśaṅkhatuṣārābhaṁ kṣīrodārṇavasambhavam.
Namāmi Śaśinaṁ Somaṁ Śambhormukuṭabhūṣaṇam.||2||

|| Maṅgala Śānti Mantra ||
Om Krāṁ Krīṁ Krauṁ Saḥ Bhaumāya Namaḥ.
Dharaṇīgarbhasambhūtaṁ vidyutkāntisamaprabham.
Kumāraṁ śaktihastaṁ taṁ Maṅgalaṁ praṇamāmyaham.||3||

|| Budha Śānti Mantra ||
Om Brāṁ Brīṁ Brauṁ Saḥ Budhāya Namaḥ.
Priyaṅgukalikāśyāmaṁ rūpeṇāpratimaṁ Budham.
Saumyaṁ saumyaguṇopetaṁ taṁ Budhaṁ praṇamāmyaham.||4||

|| Guru Śānti Mantra ||
Om Grāṁ Grīṁ Grauṁ Saḥ Gurave Namaḥ.
Devānāṁ cha ṛṣīṇāṁ cha guruṁ kāñchanasannibham.
Buddhibhūtaṁ trilokeśaṁ taṁ namāmi Bṛhaspatim.||5||

|| Śukra Śānti Mantra ||
Om Drāṁ Drīṁ Drauṁ Saḥ Śukrāya Namaḥ.
Himakundamṛṇālābhaṁ daityānāṁ paramaṁ gurum.
Sarvaśāstrapravaktāraṁ Bhārgavaṁ praṇamāmyaham.||6||

|| Śani Śānti Mantra ||
Om Prāṁ Prīṁ Prauṁ Saḥ Śanaiścharāya Namaḥ.
Nīlāñjanasamābhāsaṁ Raviputraṁ Yamāgrajam.
Chhāyāmārtaṇḍasambhūtaṁ taṁ namāmi Śanaiścharam.||7||

|| Rāhu Śānti Mantra ||
Om Bhrāṁ Bhrīṁ Bhrauṁ Saḥ Rāhave Namaḥ.
Ardhakāyaṁ mahāvīryaṁ chandrādityavimardanam.
Siṁhikāgarbhasambhūtaṁ taṁ Rāhuṁ praṇamāmyaham.||8||

|| Ketu Śānti Mantra ||
Om Srāṁ Srīṁ Srauṁ Saḥ Ketave Namaḥ.
Palāśapuṣpasaṅkāśaṁ tārakāgrahamastakam.
Raudraṁ raudrātmakaṁ ghoraṁ taṁ Ketuṁ praṇamāmyaham.||9||

|| Sāmūhika Śānti Mantra ||
Brahmā Murāristripurāntakārī
Bhānuḥ Śaśī Bhūmisuto Budhaścha.
Guruścha Śukraḥ Śanirāhuketavaḥ
Sarve grāhāḥ śāntikarāḥ bhavantu.||10||

Om Sarvagrāhebhyo Namaḥ. Om Śāntiḥ Śāntiḥ Śāntiḥ.

|| Iti Shrī Graha Śānti Mantra sampūrṇaḥ ||
hindiMeaning: सभी नौ ग्रहों की शान्ति के लिए सामूहिक मन्त्र संग्रह: (1) सूर्य - गुड़हल पुष्प जैसे, कश्यप के पुत्र, महान तेज वाले, अन्धकार के शत्रु, सर्वपापघ्न दिवाकर को प्रणाम। (2) चन्द्र - दही, शंख, तुषार जैसी आभा वाले, क्षीरसागर से उत्पन्न, शम्भु के मुकुट भूषण सोम को नमस्कार। (3) मंगल - धरणी के गर्भ से उत्पन्न, विद्युत के समान कान्ति, कुमार, शक्तिहस्त मंगल को प्रणाम। (4) बुध - प्रियंगु कली जैसे श्याम, अप्रतिम रूप, सौम्यगुण वाले बुध को प्रणाम। (5) गुरु - देवों-ऋषियों के गुरु, स्वर्ण जैसी कान्ति, बुद्धिस्वरूप, त्रिलोकेश बृहस्पति को नमस्कार। (6) शुक्र - हिमकुन्द मृणाल जैसे, दैत्यगुरु, सर्वशास्त्रप्रवक्ता भार्गव को प्रणाम। (7) शनि - नीले अंजन जैसे, रविपुत्र, यम के अग्रज शनैश्चर को नमस्कार। (8) राहु - अर्धकाय, महावीर्य, चन्द्र-सूर्य को ग्रसने वाले, सिंहिका के गर्भ से उत्पन्न राहु को प्रणाम। (9) केतु - पलाश पुष्प जैसे, तारकाग्रह, रौद्र, घोर केतु को प्रणाम। (10) सभी ग्रह शान्तिकर हों।
benefits: Complete peace mantras for all nine planets in one collection
Each graha has its specific bija mantra and dhyana shloka
Collective recitation pacifies all planetary afflictions simultaneously
Contains the famous Navagraha dhyana shlokas from Navagraha Stotra
Ideal for daily recitation during any planetary dasha or transit
Concludes with the powerful Samuhika Shanti mantra for universal peace
description: Graha Shanti Mantra — ग्रह शान्ति मन्त्र — is a sacred prayer offered to the Nine Planets (Navagraha). This revered text originates from Vedic Jyotish Tradition. Regular recitation is believed to complete peace mantras for all nine planets in one collection, contain the famous Navagraha dhyana shlokas from Navagraha Stotra, and collective recitation pacifies all planetary afflictions simultaneously. Comprising 10 verses, it can be completed in approximately 8 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Graha Shanti Mantra in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Graha Shanti Mantra - collective peace mantras for all 9 planets in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. For planetary remedy and universal peace.
relatedSearches: Graha Shanti Mantra lyrics Sanskrit Devanagari PDF
Navagraha mantras with bija mantras list
Surya Chandra Mangal Budh Guru Shukra Shani Rahu Ketu mantras
Navagraha homam puja vidhi at home
How many times to chant graha mantras japa count
Mangal dosha kuja dosha remedies mantras

seoFaqs.question: What is the Graha Shanti Mantra collection and what does it contain?
seoFaqs.answer: The Graha Shanti Mantra is a comprehensive Sanskrit collection bringing together the bija mantras and dhyana shlokas of all nine planets (Navagraha) into a single sadhana. The collection opens with Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya and a sankalpa for sarvagrahanam shantaye (peace of all planets). It then provides for each of the nine grahas: (1) the trayakshara bija mantra in the Hraam-Hreem-Hraum Sah pattern, (2) the planet-specific tarpana bija (Suryaya namah, Chandraya namah, etc.), and (3) the classical dhyana shloka from Vyasa's Navagraha Stotra (Japa-kusuma-sankasham for Surya, Dadhi-shankha-tushar-abham for Chandra, etc.). It concludes with the Brahma Murari Tripurantakari samuhika shanti mantra invoking all nine simultaneously. (Sources: Vedic Jyotish Tradition; Vyasa Navagraha Stotra; Hindupedia Navagraha.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the bija mantras of the Navagraha?
seoFaqs.answer: Each of the nine planets has a tri-syllabic bija (seed) mantra in the Hraam-Hreem-Hraum Sah formula prescribed by the Mantra Mahodadhi: Surya — Om Hraam Hreem Hraum Sah Suryaya Namah; Chandra — Om Shraam Shreem Shraum Sah Chandraya Namah; Mangal — Om Kraam Kreem Kraum Sah Bhaumaya Namah; Budha — Om Braam Breem Braum Sah Budhaya Namah; Guru — Om Graam Greem Graum Sah Gurave Namah; Shukra — Om Draam Dreem Draum Sah Shukraya Namah; Shani — Om Praam Preem Praum Sah Shanaishcharaya Namah; Rahu — Om Bhraam Bhreem Bhraum Sah Rahave Namah; Ketu — Om Sraam Sreem Sraum Sah Ketave Namah. Each bija is japad in the prescribed count for that planet. (Sources: Mantra Mahodadhi; Vedic Jyotish texts; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: How many times should each Navagraha mantra be chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: The classical japa-sankhya (chanting count) for each graha mantra is prescribed in the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra and Mantra Mahodadhi: Surya 7,000; Chandra 11,000; Mangal 10,000; Budha 9,000 (some texts 4,000); Guru 19,000 (some 16,000); Shukra 16,000; Shani 23,000 (some 19,000); Rahu 18,000; Ketu 17,000. These counts represent the full purascharana for each planet, typically completed across forty-eight days. For daily shanti, devotees chant 11/27/108 of each, or focus on the specific afflicted graha as identified in the kundali. The total adds to approximately 1.4 lakh japas for the full Navagraha purascharana. (Sources: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra; Mantra Mahodadhi; Hindupedia Navagraha sadhana.)

seoFaqs.question: When and on which days should Graha Shanti Mantras be chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: Each graha has its specific weekday: Sunday (Surya), Monday (Chandra), Tuesday (Mangal), Wednesday (Budha), Thursday (Guru), Friday (Shukra), Saturday (Shani). Rahu and Ketu have no specific weekdays but are associated with Saturday and Tuesday respectively, and Rahu-kala (each day's 90-minute Rahu period) for special remedies. The collective Graha Shanti Mantra is best chanted on Saturdays (Shani being the slowest and most karma-affecting graha), during eclipses (when planetary energies intensify), at the start of every dasha or antardasha period, on transit changes (gochara) of major planets, on the 27 Nakshatra days corresponding to one's janma-nakshatra, and during Navagraha Homas. (Sources: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra; Drikpanchang weekday lords; Vedic Jyotish.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Graha Shanti Mantra recitation grant?
seoFaqs.answer: Collective Graha Shanti recitation pacifies all nine planetary afflictions simultaneously, neutralising the negative effects of mahadasha and antardasha periods, transit doshas (sade-sati for Saturn, Mangal-dosha, Rahu-Ketu peyarchi, Guru-chandala-yoga, Shukra-Shani sambandha), birth-chart afflictions, planetary war (graha-yuddha) periods, and eclipse karmic exposure. Specific benefits include health (Surya-Chandra), confidence (Mangal), intellect (Budha), wisdom (Guru), prosperity and relationships (Shukra), removal of obstacles and longevity (Shani), removal of mysterious disturbances (Rahu), and spiritual liberation (Ketu). The samuhika mantra at the end is said to grant universal peace (sarvashaantikara) for the entire household. (Sources: Mantra Mahodadhi phalashruti; Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Navagraha Stotra and Graha Shanti Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: These two Navagraha compositions serve different functions. The Navagraha Stotra (commonly attributed to Vyasa Maharshi) is the classical 9-verse Sanskrit hymn containing only the dhyana shlokas of each planet (Japa-kusuma-sankasham for Surya, etc.) — used for daily darshana and as part of Navagraha temple worship at temples like Suryanar Kovil, Thirunallar (Shani sthala), and the famous Navagraha Stalas of Tamil Nadu. The Graha Shanti Mantra is a more comprehensive sadhana-collection adding the trayakshara bija mantras and the tarpana mantras for each graha, used for active dosha-shanti and homa rituals. The Navagraha Stotra is for sthana-darshana, the Graha Shanti Mantra for active remedy. (Sources: Vyasa Navagraha Stotra; Mantra Mahodadhi; Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.)

seoFaqs.question: Are there specific samagri offerings for each Navagraha?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes — each graha has its prescribed grain (dhanya), color, gemstone, metal, flower, and naivedya for shanti rituals: Surya — wheat, red, ruby, copper, red lotus, jaggery; Chandra — rice, white, pearl, silver, jasmine, milk-rice; Mangal — masur dal, red, coral, copper, red flowers, jaggery-rice; Budha — moong dal, green, emerald, brass, vishnukranti, milk; Guru — chana dal, yellow, yellow sapphire, gold, yellow flowers, kheer; Shukra — white beans, white, diamond, silver, white flowers, sweets; Shani — black sesame, black/blue, blue sapphire, iron, blue flowers, sesame oil; Rahu — urad dal, smoke-grey, hessonite, lead, dark flowers, coconut; Ketu — kulthi dal, multicolored, cat's eye, mixed metal, mixed flowers, banana. These are offered during the corresponding bija mantra recitation. (Sources: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra; Hindupedia Navagraha samagri.)
